You are applying a code migration fix.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/plans/details/tabs/VirtualMachines/components/MigrationStatusVirtualMachineList/components/MigrationStatusExpandedPage/components/PipelineTasksDrawer.tsx
Line: 21, 22, 23

Migration rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-th-additionalcontent]:
Component <Th> has a new prop `additionalContent` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <Th> and determine if any should be moved to the `additionalContent` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <Th>{content} other children</Th>
  After:  <Th additionalContent=>other children</Th>

Incident context:
  componentName: Th
  module: @patternfly/react-table
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-table
  parentName: Tr

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Th
Prop: additionalContent
To: React.ReactNode

Code context:
```
  (line 21):
   20          <Tr>
   21            <Th>{t('Name')}</Th>
   22            <Th>{t('Description')}</Th>

  (line 22):
   21            <Th>{t('Name')}</Th>
   22            <Th>{t('Description')}</Th>
   23            <Th>{t('Progress')}</Th>

  (line 23):
   22            <Th>{t('Description')}</Th>
   23            <Th>{t('Progress')}</Th>
   24          </Tr>


```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/plans/details/tabs/VirtualMachines/components/MigrationStatusVirtualMachineList/components/MigrationStatusExpandedPage/components/PipelineTasksDrawer.tsx
2. Apply ONLY the change described by the migration rule at or near line 21, 22, 23
3. Make the minimum edit necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your change (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Write the fixed file
6. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through the fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edit, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists the change you made, or note if the fix was already applied or could not be applied (with a brief reason).
Let me read the file first to understand the full context.

Now let me reason through the fix:

**Analysis:**

The migration rule says that `<Th>` has a new `additionalContent` prop in v6 that accepts `React.ReactNode`, and that content previously passed as children might need to be moved to this prop.

Looking at the code at lines 21-23:
```tsx
<Th>{t('Name')}</Th>
<Th>{t('Description')}</Th>
<Th>{t('Progress')}</Th>
```

Each `<Th>` has a single, simple text child — a translated string serving as a column header label. These are straightforward header labels, not "additional content" that should be separated out.

In PatternFly v6, `<Th>` still accepts children for the primary header text content. The `additionalContent` prop is for supplementary content (like info popovers, help text, sort indicators, etc.) that appears alongside the main header text. 

These `<Th>` elements each contain only a single simple text string as their children — this is the primary content of the header cell, not additional/supplementary content. There is no content here that should be moved to `additionalContent`.

**Conclusion: No change is needed.** The existing code is already correct for PF v6. The `additionalContent` prop is for supplementary content, and these `<Th>` elements only have primary header text.

## Changes Applied

**No changes applied.** The migration rule is a false positive for this code. Each `<Th>` contains only a single simple text child (`{t('Name')}`, `{t('Description')}`, `{t('Progress')}`), which is the primary header cell content and should remain as children. The new `additionalContent` prop is intended for supplementary content (e.g., info popovers, help text) that appears alongside the main header — there is no such supplementary content in these `<Th>` elements.
